command:
TASK PACKET: env-scrub (roadmap item 2 — scrub orchestrator env vars from child agent processes)

OBJECTIVE
Agent subprocesses currently inherit the worker's FULL environment. In
backend/src/infra/runtime/cli_runner.py there are three `_env()` implementations that
spread `**os.environ` (around lines 506 (pi), 549 (claude), 593 (gemini)). This leaks
ORCHESTRATOR_HOME, PROJECT_REPO_DIR, ORCHESTRATOR_MASTER_KEY, ORCHESTRATOR_API_TOKEN and
other worker-only variables into child coding agents — one of the two breadcrumbs that let
an exploring agent find and write to the project main repo.

Replace the `**os.environ` spread with a single shared allowlist helper in the same module,
e.g. `def _base_child_env() -> dict[str, str]`, that copies ONLY these vars from os.environ
when present: PATH, HOME, USER, LOGNAME, SHELL, LANG, LC_ALL, LC_CTYPE, TERM, TMPDIR, TZ,
plus XDG_CONFIG_HOME, XDG_DATA_HOME, XDG_CACHE_HOME (CLI config discovery). Each runner's
`_env()` then returns `{**_base_child_env(), <its api key var>: self._api_key}` (pi keeps
its PI_BACKEND_ENV_VAR[self._backend] key var lookup exactly as-is).

CONSTRAINTS
- MUST NOT restructure the PI_BACKEND_ENV_VAR mapping or RUNTIME_TYPES — the shared-abstractions
  ledger flags that as a registry redesign requiring user escalation. Only consume it as today.
- MUST keep `correlation_env()` (cli_runner.py:65) as the only source of ORCHESTRATOR_* correlation
  ids; those are merged separately at the call site (line ~375 `env={**self._env(), **execution_env}`)
  and must keep working.
- The `os.environ.get("ORCHESTRATOR_HOME", ...)` read around line 173 is worker-side config
  resolution, NOT child env construction — leave it alone.
- Do not modify any file outside backend/src/infra/runtime/cli_runner.py and backend/tests/.
- No domain (backend/src/domain) or app (backend/src/app) changes. Follow existing code style;
  structlog only, no print/logging.

TESTS (required)
Add/extend unit tests (backend/tests/unit/, mirror existing runtime runner test layout — look at
existing tests for these runners first) asserting for each of the three runners:
- ORCHESTRATOR_HOME, PROJECT_REPO_DIR, ORCHESTRATOR_MASTER_KEY, ORCHESTRATOR_API_TOKEN set in
  os.environ (use monkeypatch) do NOT appear in the constructed child env;
- PATH and HOME DO appear; the runner's API key var appears with the right value;
- absent allowlisted vars are simply omitted (no KeyError).

ACCEPTANCE / VERIFICATION (will be run externally)
- grep: no `**os.environ` remains in cli_runner.py subprocess env construction.
- cd backend && python -m pytest tests/unit -q  → all pass
- ruff check src tests → clean; mypy src → zero errors.

Work ONLY in this directory (it is an isolated git worktree):
/workspaces/agent-orchestrator/.orchestrator/worktrees/env-scrub
Leave your changes UNCOMMITTED in the working tree. Do not run git commit, do not touch git config.
Reply with exactly one word: OK

command:
TASK PACKET: intent-doc (roadmap item 5, first bullet — documentation of EXISTING behavior only).

Work ONLY inside this directory (an isolated git worktree): /workspaces/agent-orchestrator/.orchestrator/worktrees/intent-doc
Leave changes UNCOMMITTED. Do not run git commit.

OBJECTIVE: The IntentProposal concept is implemented but not written down anywhere. Add a written definition to the most fitting file under docs/architecture/ (read docs/architecture/plan-lifecycle.md and docs/architecture/data-model.md first and pick ONE; add a short cross-reference in the other only if natural).

The definition must state, verified against the code (cite nothing you cannot confirm by reading it):
- IntentProposal is a DOMAIN entity (backend/src/domain/entities/planning_artifacts.py, around lines 99-111), not an API DTO; the API currently surfaces the domain model directly as the OpenAPI schema (backend/src/api/routers/plans.py — PlanDetailResponse field `intent_proposal`).
- Its `revision` starts at 1; the Plan aggregate enforces monotonic +1 increments on revise (backend/src/domain/aggregates/planner_orchestrator.py ~702-703) and exact-revision approval (~729-730) — a stale revision raises InvalidEditError.
- This exact-revision gate is the optimistic-concurrency mechanism for the HUMAN review gate, and is distinct from Plan.version CAS (which guards worker-vs-edit races).
- Where it sits in the cycle lifecycle: IntentProposal (versioned, exact-revision review gate) -> CycleDraft -> active Cycle.

CONSTRAINTS: modify ONLY files under docs/architecture/ in the worktree. Match the surrounding docs' tone and structure (read them first). Do not invent behavior; if the code contradicts anything above, describe what the code actually does. Do not edit ROADMAP.md, backend/, or decision logs.

When done, print a summary under 100 words: file(s) changed, section added, and any discrepancy you found between this packet and the code.
